onboarding.question:
1. Define what happens to the user experience when the AI is unavailable during a logged-out exploration session — should the user see an error message, a retry affordance, or be blocked from further interaction?
2. Specify the exact inactivity threshold in minutes at which the session expiry warning banner should appear (e.g., 5 minutes before expiration).
3. Define the exact session timeout duration in minutes for unauthenticated users before their logged-out session expires.
4. Specify the exact observable trigger or condition that causes the "Not signed in" indicator to disappear after successful authentication (e.g., within one render cycle, immediately upon token receipt, or after conversation history loads).
5. Resolve the conflict between AC#8 (non-dismissible warning banner) and the referenced modal design — clarify whether the session expiry warning should be a non-dismissible banner at the top of chat or a modal overlay.
6. Clarify the distinction between the "default logged-out state" and the "
onboarding.recommendations:
[1]. My recommendation: When the AI is unavailable during logged-out exploration, an inline error message appears at the top of the chat interface stating "The AI is currently unavailable. Please try again in a moment." with no persistent retry button — the user can attempt to send another message to retry automatically; the chat interface remains interactive.

→ Rationale: An inline error keeps chrome minimal and respects the exploration principle; automatic retry on next message avoids creating a persistent error state; remaining interactive preserves user agency.

→ Note: Pending your approval — say yes to apply to the product spec

---

[2]. My recommendation: The session expiry warning banner appears when the inactivity timer reaches 10 minutes remaining (50 minutes of inactivity elapsed out of a 60-minute total session).

→ Rationale: 10 minutes remaining provides sufficient lead time for the user to complete SSO sign-up before expiry; it balances warning frequency with actionability.

→ Note: Pending your approval — say yes to apply to the product spec

---

[3]. My recommendation: Unauthenticated sessions expire after 60 minutes of inactivity from the last user action.

→ Rationale: 60 minutes aligns with industry standard session durations and provides adequate exploration time without creating unbounded session state; it is aligned with AC#22 which already specifies this duration.

→ Note: Pending your approval — say yes to apply to the product spec

---

[4]. My recommendation: The "Not signed in" indicator disappears the moment authentication state is confirmed on the client (immediately upon valid token receipt), before conversation history loads; conversation history then appears progressively without the indicator present.

→ Rationale: Removing the indicator immediately upon token receipt signals authentication success to the user without delay; progressively loading history afterward maintains perceived speed while preserving the visual clarity of state transition.

→ Note: Pending your approval — say yes to apply to the product spec

---

[5]. My recommendation: The session expiry warning is a non-dismissible banner at the top of the chat interface — not a modal overlay; it remains visible until the user taps "Sign in now" or their inactivity timer is reset by an action (which clears it automatically).

→ Rationale: A banner is less intrusive than a modal and aligns with the minimalist design principle; it keeps the user in chat context and allows them to continue exploring while being warned; non-dismissibility ensures the warning is not accidentally ignored.

→ Note: Pending your approval — say yes to apply to the product spec

---

[6]. My recommendation: The blocking items list appears to be incomplete or corrupted. Confirm whether item 6 is the actual blocking question or if there are additional items the designer needs resolved before moving to engineering handoff.

→ Rationale: Item 6 cuts off mid-sentence with "Clarify the distinction between the 'default logged-out state' and the '" — I cannot provide a recommendation without the complete question.

→ Note: Awaiting clarification

---

Say *yes* to apply these to the product spec and continue design, or reply to adjust any recommendation.
